stocks.recent_news: Q4/FY26 results declared May 6, 2026: Q4 revenue Rs 3,128.5 cr and PAT Rs 411.6 cr; FY26 EPS Rs 7.71 broadly in line with estimates. Q4 order intake Rs 5,335 cr (+39% YoY); unexecuted order book Rs 17,107 cr (+61% YoY) as of March 31, 2026. Stock hit all-time high of Rs 874.20 on May 8, 2026 (+7.4% post-results); up ~18% over the past month. Analyst concall held May 6.
stocks.sector_context: Capital goods / T&D sector in strong upcycle. India transformer market projected ~8.3% CAGR (USD 3B in 2025 to USD 4.5B by 2030). Industry capacity expanding from 375 GVA (FY25) to ~690 GVA by FY28E with listed-player capex of ~Rs 66B. Near-term demand sufficient to absorb expanded supply without pricing pressure.
stocks.peer_comparison: Order book vs T&D peers: Hitachi Energy Rs 29,872 cr, Siemens Energy Rs 17,599 cr, CG Power Rs 17,107 cr, GE Vernova T&D Rs 14,380 cr, ABB Rs 10,471 cr. CG Power's order book growth (+61% YoY) is among the strongest. FY26 sales growth 21.25% vs sector average mid-teens.
red_flags: Stock at all-time high (Rs 874.20) - consensus target Rs 818 sits below CMP, implying limited near-term upside
Promoter group reclassification effective March 18, 2026 (Algavista Greentech, Coromandel Engineering, Yanmar Coromandel moved from Promoter Group to Public)
Q4 results in-line rather than beat - fresh order wins needed to justify multiple expansion
catalysts: Large order wins given Rs 17,107 cr book (+61% YoY)
PowerGrid/SEB capex cycle and HVDC programs

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results already announced April 29, 2026 (in price). Board meets May 29, 2026 for FY26 final dividend and investor conference. AP Polymers merger into Asian Paints became effective March 31, 2026; subsidiary dissolved. Stock at Rs 2,599.90 as of May 8, 2026. Q3 FY26 reference revenue was Rs 8,578 cr; Q4 consensus was Rs 8,500-9,000 cr.
stocks.sector_context: Paint/decoratives sector facing intensified competition from Birla Opus (Grasim) and JSW Paints, eroding pricing power and historical premium multiples. Asian Paints sits in Nifty 500 (weight 0.62%) but not in Nifty FMCG; trades with consumer discretionary cohort.
stocks.peer_comparison: 12-month consensus target band Rs 2,400-2,950 (split Hold/Buy) implies modest upside from Rs 2,600. Underperforming Nifty FMCG which outshone Nifty50 in April 2026. Competitive intensity capping multiples vs historical premium that Asian Paints commanded as category leader.
red_flags: Heightened competitive intensity from Birla Opus and JSW Paints
Market share erosion risk in decorative paints segment
Analyst consensus is split (Hold/Buy) rather than uniformly bullish
catalysts: FY27 guidance commentary at investor conference

stocks.recent_news: FY26 audited results approved May 7, 2026: consolidated net profit up 19.28% YoY to Rs 1,089.40 cr; net sales up 11.17% to Rs 16,811.65 cr. Final dividend Rs 6.50/share recommended (payable on/after Aug 14, 2026). Ashish Bharat Ram reappointed as director. AGM scheduled August 11, 2026. Investor concall held May 7. Stock up ~7.44% over the last month; market cap ~Rs 95,125 cr.
stocks.sector_context: Auto ancillary/forgings sector benefiting from defence ordering momentum, recovery in US Class 8 truck cycle, and India manufacturing capex. Management bullish on defence vertical and aerospace.
stocks.peer_comparison: FY27 management guidance of 25% revenue growth (with EBITDA expansion) for India manufacturing operations is above sector average growth expectations. One-month performance (+7.44%) outpaces broad auto ancillary index.
red_flags: FY27 25% revenue growth is aggressive guidance - execution risk if Class 8 truck cycle softens
Export exposure to US/Europe creates currency and demand-cycle risk
catalysts: Final dividend record date and payment August 14, 2026

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results (April 22, 2026): revenue Rs 4,056 cr, PAT Rs 529 cr (+34% YoY, +20% QoQ). FY26 revenue $1,654.4M (+17.4% YoY), EBIT margin 15.6%; total FY26 dividend Rs 40/share (vs Rs 35 in FY25). May 5, 2026: investor/analyst sessions held. May 7, 2026: earned Databricks Brickbuilder Specialization for Healthcare & Life Sciences; announced AI Innovation Lab partnership with IIT Kharagpur.
stocks.sector_context: Nifty IT down ~25% YTD CY2026 (vs Nifty50 down ~10%) on fears of AI/agentic disruption to traditional IT services model. Sector at 30-month lows. CLSA notes no AI-led pricing pressure in Q4 deal renewals and pipelines intact, but sentiment remains weak. Vertical demand commentary broadly stable.
stocks.peer_comparison: Persistent posted sector-leading growth (FY26 +17.4%) vs single-digit growth at TCS/Infosys/HCLTech, yet trades at premium PE of ~42.2 (vs TCS ~17x, Infosys ~16x). DSIJ flagged the 'strong numbers, falling stock' disconnect - best-in-class fundamentals being dragged by sector derating.
red_flags: Premium PE (~42.2) vulnerable to further sector multiple compression
Sector-wide AI disruption narrative weighing on sentiment despite intact deal pipelines
Nifty IT at 30-month lows; momentum traders avoiding the space
Mid-cap IT names tend to derate more sharply than large-cap when sector sentiment turns
catalysts: Databricks Brickbuilder and IIT Kharagpur AI Lab monetization
FY27 deal pipeline and TCV visibility
Final dividend record date (Rs 18/share component of Rs 40 FY26 total)
Any sector sentiment reversal would benefit best-growth name disproportionately

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results announced May 7, 2026: consolidated PAT up 15.75% YoY to Rs 362 cr; revenue up 7.3% to Rs 3,038 cr. India FMCG business grew 9.5% in Q4. International business +2.5% (Sub-Saharan Africa +20%, Bangladesh +22%, UK/EU +10%, Namaste USA +6.2%; Middle East soft). Final dividend Rs 5.50/share (total FY26 dividend 825% = Rs 975.5 cr). FY26 revenue Rs 13,792 cr (+5% vs +1.3% in FY25). Management raised India revenue guidance from high single-digit to low double-digit for FY27. Stock gained ~4% on May 8 post-results.
stocks.sector_context: Nifty FMCG outperformed Nifty50 in April 2026; broad-based strength across HUL, Emami, Colgate, Dabur (all +1-4% in late-April session). Sector tailwinds from rural demand recovery and benign input costs.
stocks.peer_comparison: Dabur's 9.5% India FMCG growth in Q4 is among the strongest in the cohort, ahead of HUL and Colgate's more muted prints. Weight in Nifty FMCG 3.53%. Growth inflection from FY25 +1.3% to FY26 +5% (with FY27 guidance of low double-digit) is the clearest re-rating catalyst in the FMCG basket.
red_flags: International business soft at +2.5%, dragged by Middle East geopolitics
Revenue growth still trailing some peers on full-year basis
catalysts: Final dividend Rs 5.50/share record date and payment
FY27 low double-digit India growth guidance execution
Healthcare and Hair Care portfolio momentum
